aiResult.chart_summary: 68-year-old female with adjustment disorder, generalized anxiety disorder, recurrent mild major depressive disorder, and insomnia presented for individual therapy follow-up. Patient reports overall stability with focus on family communication challenges and interpersonal relationship concerns; mental status exam shows cooperative attitude, appropriate affect, no suicidal/homicidal ideation, with good insight and judgment.
aiResult.top_concern: Family communication stress and need for boundary-setting skills with daughter, though patient is stable with good insight

aiResult.chart_summary: 56-year-old female with PTSD presenting for individual therapy follow-up after 2-month interval. Patient reports recent panic attack related to workplace stressor involving previous coworker, but overall functioning well with new team; provider planning to reincorporate EMDR therapy now that primary threat has relocated.
aiResult.top_concern: Recent panic attack episode indicating acute PTSD symptom exacerbation despite overall stability

aiResult.chart_summary: 76-year-old female with generalized anxiety disorder and panic attacks, experiencing 3 panic attacks since initial visit one month ago, complicated by Parkinson's disease and non-compliance with PRN anxiety medication due to family interference. Patient demonstrates good insight and judgment, reports triggers related to technology difficulties, and has upcoming psychiatric evaluation scheduled.
aiResult.top_concern: Medication non-compliance due to family influence from non-medical professionals advising against prescribed PRN anxiety medication

aiResult.chart_summary: 41-year-old female with PTSD, POTS, and panic disorder with agoraphobia presenting for virtual individual therapy session. Patient reports doing fairly well overall, managing housing transition stress, with no suicidal/homicidal ideation, cooperative attitude, and appropriate affect though worried mood noted.
aiResult.top_concern: Patient reports limited emotional bandwidth and difficulty with regular communication, combined with history of falls and ongoing management of multiple psychiatric conditions including PTSD and panic disorder with agoraphobia

aiResult.chart_summary: 54-year-old male with major depressive disorder in partial remission, traumatic brain injury with sequelae including dementia and memory deficits from 2014 MVA, currently in maintenance therapy. PHQ-9 screening shows ongoing depressive symptoms including sleep disturbance nearly every day, low mood more than half the days, and concentration difficulties, though patient denies overt depressive symptoms and reports staying busy at work.
aiResult.top_concern: Discrepancy between patient's self-report of no overt depressive symptoms and PHQ-9 score of moderate depression with significant sleep, mood, and concentration issues, possibly indicating poor insight related to TBI-associated cognitive deficits

aiResult.chart_summary: 41-year-old female with chronic major depressive disorder, PTSD, and generalized anxiety disorder presented for individual therapy follow-up. Patient reports feeling fairly good overall with passive suicidal ideation (no intent/plan/means), currently off work for 10 days, expressing concern about seasonal worsening of depressive symptoms during winter months.
aiResult.top_concern: Passive suicidal ideation present despite reporting overall improvement in mood and functioning

aiResult.chart_summary: 28-year-old female presenting to establish care with primary concern of inability to lose weight despite 2 years of exercise and healthy diet attempts. Vital signs show BMI of 40.85 (Class III obesity) with otherwise normal screening results including negative depression, intimate partner violence, and alcohol screenings.
aiResult.top_concern: Class III obesity (BMI 40.85) with failed conservative weight management despite 2-year effort

aiResult.chart_summary: 21-year-old male presents to urgent care with a 2-day history of dry cough following exposure to friends with bronchitis. Physical examination is unremarkable with normal vital signs, clear lungs, and no fever; diagnosed with acute viral cough and managed conservatively with supportive care recommendations.
aiResult.top_concern: Abnormally low temperature of 95.9°F (tympanic) may indicate measurement error or early illness, though patient appears well with otherwise normal vital signs

aiResult.chart_summary: 21-year-old male college student presents with 5 days of progressively worsening severe sore throat affecting swallowing and sleep, with bilateral tonsillar hypertrophy and erythema on exam. Negative strep test earlier in week; treated empirically with Augmentin and prednisone for possible false-negative strep versus mononucleosis.
aiResult.top_concern: Significant pharyngeal swelling with potential airway compromise risk and possible mononucleosis with associated complications if diagnosis delayed

aiResult.chart_summary: 67-year-old female presenting with 5-day viral upper respiratory infection that progressed from sore throat to productive cough with green sputum disrupting sleep. Patient has clear lung sounds without fever or dyspnea; prescribed azithromycin to start if not improved by Monday and benzonatate for cough suppression.
aiResult.top_concern: Patient has significant cardiovascular risk factors including history of TIA, elevated coronary calcium score, family history of MI, and current tachycardia (pulse 104) with secondhand smoke exposure at home

aiResult.chart_summary: 15-month-old female presenting for routine well-child visit with recurrent diaper rash as chief concern. Patient received age-appropriate immunizations (DTaP, Hib, PCV-15, Influenza) and mupirocin prescribed for diaper rash; developmental milestones appropriate for age with minor concern about language regression.
aiResult.top_concern: Language regression - child previously knew at least 4 words but now only saying 'all done' for everything, requires monitoring and follow-up in 1 month

aiResult.chart_summary: 3-year-11-month-old female presenting with hand, foot, and mouth disease (HFMD) characterized by vesicular rash on hands, feet, ankles, and buttocks that began Friday, preceded by fever on Wednesday. Patient is well-appearing, adequately hydrated, eating and drinking well, with minimal oral involvement and no current fever.
aiResult.top_concern: None - routine viral illness with appropriate supportive care plan and good hydration status

aiResult.chart_summary: 19-year-old female college student presenting with acute viral pharyngitis characterized by severe sore throat, tonsillar hypertrophy with exudates, fever, and cervical lymphadenopathy for 3 days. Strep, influenza, COVID, and RSV testing negative; treated with prednisone for tonsillar swelling and supportive care with antipyretics.
aiResult.top_concern: Significant tonsillar hypertrophy with 3+ enlargement and exudates requiring steroids, with patient reporting worsening symptoms and appearing tearful - needs close monitoring for airway compromise and strict return precautions

aiResult.chart_summary: 23-year-old primigravida at 29 weeks gestation with recurrent E. coli UTIs (3 episodes requiring multiple antibiotic courses), low ferritin (9), and history of frequent headaches. Patient has anterior placenta, passed GDM screening, and is currently taking prenatal vitamins and magnesium with good fetal movement noted.
aiResult.top_concern: Recurrent UTI with persistent E. coli despite multiple antibiotic treatments (Keflex, Macrobid) requiring close monitoring for pyelonephritis and preterm labor

aiResult.chart_summary: 33-year-old primigravida at 25+ weeks gestation with complex medical history including chronic migraines requiring multiple medications (Fioricet, sumatriptan), generalized anxiety disorder on Lexapro, BMI 36+, and significant concern about weight gain (43 lbs total gain). Family history concerning for fetal cardiac defects and Tourette syndrome requiring MFM consultation with fetal echocardiogram completed showing normal results.
aiResult.top_concern: Escalating migraine frequency requiring increasing Fioricet use (multiple times per week vs once weekly in second trimester) with potential for medication rebound headaches and narcotic exposure, combined with excessive gestational weight gain (43 lbs at 25 weeks) in context of obesity and eating disorder concerns

aiResult.chart_summary: 26-year-old woman at 9 weeks gestation with complex obstetric history including previous termination for Down syndrome with cardiac defects, history of SGA infant, and ongoing substance use (THC positive, nicotine). Multiple comorbidities including BMI 35.9, pelvic organ prolapse, GERD, anxiety/depression history, and continued cannabis/nicotine use despite pregnancy.
aiResult.top_concern: Continued marijuana and nicotine use during pregnancy with history of previous child with Down syndrome/cardiac defects and SGA infant, now referred to maternal-fetal medicine for high-risk monitoring
